Modelowanie tematĂłw LDA

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)

Wymagane pakiety

# Wymagane pakiety ----
library(tm)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(topicmodels)
library(wordcloud)

0. Funkcja top_terms_by_topic_LDA

# 0. Funkcja top_terms_by_topic_LDA ----
# ktĂłra wczytuje tekst 
# (wektor lub kolumna tekstowa z ramki danych)
# i wizualizuje sĹ‚owa o najwiÄ™kszej informatywnoĹ›ci
# przy metody uĹĽyciu LDA
# dla wyznaczonej liczby tematĂłw



top_terms_by_topic_LDA <- function(input_text, # wektor lub kolumna tekstowa z ramki danych
                                   plot = TRUE, # domyĹ›lnie rysuje wykres
                                   k = number_of_topics) # wyznaczona liczba k tematĂłw
{    
  corpus <- VCorpus(VectorSource(input_text))
  DTM <- DocumentTermMatrix(corpus)
  
  # usuĹ„ wszystkie puste wiersze w macierzy czÄ™stoĹ›ci
  # poniewaĹĽ spowodujÄ… bĹ‚Ä…d dla LDA
  unique_indexes <- unique(DTM$i) # pobierz indeks kaĹĽdej unikalnej wartoĹ›ci
  DTM <- DTM[unique_indexes,]    # pobierz z DTM podzbiĂłr tylko tych unikalnych indeksĂłw
  
  # wykonaj LDA
  lda <- LDA(DTM, k = number_of_topics, control = list(seed = 1234))
  topics <- tidy(lda, matrix = "beta") # pobierz sĹ‚owa/tematy w uporzÄ…dkowanym formacie tidy
  
  # pobierz dziesiÄ™Ä‡ najczÄ™stszych sĹ‚Ăłw dla kaĹĽdego tematu
  top_terms <- topics  %>%
    group_by(topic) %>%
    top_n(10, beta) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    arrange(topic, -beta) # uporzÄ…dkuj sĹ‚owa w malejÄ…cej kolejnoĹ›ci informatywnoĹ›ci

    
  
  # rysuj wykres (domyĹ›lnie plot = TRUE)
  if(plot == T){
    # dziesiÄ™Ä‡ najczÄ™stszych sĹ‚Ăłw dla kaĹĽdego tematu
    top_terms %>%
      mutate(term = reorder(term, beta)) %>% # posortuj sĹ‚owa wedĹ‚ug wartoĹ›ci beta 
      ggplot(aes(term, beta, fill = factor(topic))) + # rysuj beta wedĹ‚ug tematu
      geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) + # wykres kolumnowy
      facet_wrap(~ topic, scales = "free") + # kaĹĽdy temat na osobnym wykresie
      labs(x = "Terminy", y = "Î˛ (waĹĽnoĹ›Ä‡ sĹ‚owa w temacie)") +
      coord_flip() +
      theme_minimal() +
      scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1")
  }else{ 
    # jeĹ›li uĹĽytkownik nie chce wykresu
    # wtedy zwrĂłÄ‡ listÄ™ posortowanych sĹ‚Ăłw
    return(top_terms)
  }
  
  
}

Dane tekstowe

# Dane tekstowe ----

# Ustaw Working Directory!
# ZaĹ‚aduj dokumenty z folderu
# docs <- DirSource("textfolder2")
# W razie potrzeby dostosuj Ĺ›cieĹĽkÄ™
# np.: docs <- DirSource("C:/User/Documents/textfolder2")


# UtwĂłrz korpus dokumentĂłw tekstowych

# Gdy tekst znajduje siÄ™ w jednym pliku csv:
data <- read.csv("LOT_reviews.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, encoding = "UTF-8")
corpus <- VCorpus(VectorSource(data$Review_Text))


# Korpus
# inspect(corpus)


# Korpus - zawartoĹ›Ä‡ przykĹ‚adowego elementu
corpus[[1]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 1155
corpus[[1]][[1]]
## [1] "Split to London Heathrow via Warsaw on 16th April 2025. Firstly, at Split airport, one has to check-in at the counter rather than online. This was absolutely fine and I was dealt with in a most professional and friendly manner by a member of the Split Airport staff, the young lady in question was both professional and friendly and a created a great impression of her company. Boarding was efficiently managed at Split airport and the aircraft used for the flight to Warsaw Chopin airport was an Embraer 190. The cabin-crew took security very seriously and made sure passengers complied. I saw cabin crew proactively checking the areas under seats and making sure baggage was stored away correctly. Transfer at Warsaw airport was efficient, LOT staff were on-hand to help passengers and alas my onward flight was delayed. However, they kept passengers well informed with text messages and announcements. The flight to London was on an immaculate Boeing 737max, the staff were exceptionally professional. A snack and coffee was offered. It is a shame there was no route map. I will certainly consider using this airline again should the opportunity arise."
corpus[[1]][2]
## $meta
##   author       : character(0)
##   datetimestamp: 2026-04-28 11:51:28.6994290351868
##   description  : character(0)
##   heading      : character(0)
##   id           : 1
##   language     : en
##   origin       : character(0)

1. Przetwarzanie i oczyszczanie tekstu

# 1. Przetwarzanie i oczyszczanie tekstu ----
# (Text Preprocessing and Text Cleaning)


# Normalizacja i usuniÄ™cie zbÄ™dnych znakĂłw ----

# Zapewnienie kodowania w caĹ‚ym korpusie
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(function(x) iconv(x, to = "UTF-8", sub = "byte")))


# Funkcja do zamiany znakĂłw na spacjÄ™
toSpace <- content_transformer(function (x, pattern) gsub(pattern, " ", x))


# UsuĹ„ zbÄ™dne znaki lub pozostaĹ‚oĹ›ci url, html itp.

# symbol @
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "@")

# symbol @ ze sĹ‚owem (zazw. nazwa uĹĽytkownika)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "@\\w+")

# linia pionowa
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "\\|")

# tabulatory
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "[ \t]{2,}")

# CAĹY adres URL:
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "(s?)(f|ht)tp(s?)://\\S+\\b")

# http i https
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "http\\w*")

# tylko ukoĹ›nik odwrotny (np. po http)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "/")

# pozostaĹ‚oĹ›Ä‡ po re-tweecie
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "(RT|via)((?:\\b\\W*@\\w+)+)")

# inne pozostaĹ‚oĹ›ci
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "www")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "~")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "Ă˘â‚¬â€ś")


# Sprawdzenie
corpus[[1]][[1]]
## [1] "Split to London Heathrow via Warsaw on 16th April 2025. Firstly, at Split airport, one has to check-in at the counter rather than online. This was absolutely fine and I was dealt with in a most professional and friendly manner by a member of the Split Airport staff, the young lady in question was both professional and friendly and a created a great impression of her company. Boarding was efficiently managed at Split airport and the aircraft used for the flight to Warsaw Chopin airport was an Embraer 190. The cabin-crew took security very seriously and made sure passengers complied. I saw cabin crew proactively checking the areas under seats and making sure baggage was stored away correctly. Transfer at Warsaw airport was efficient, LOT staff were on-hand to help passengers and alas my onward flight was delayed. However, they kept passengers well informed with text messages and announcements. The flight to London was on an immaculate Boeing 737max, the staff were exceptionally professional. A snack and coffee was offered. It is a shame there was no route map. I will certainly consider using this airline again should the opportunity arise."
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeNumbers)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, stopwords("english"))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removePunctuation)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, c("flight", "lot"))


# Sprawdzenie
corpus[[1]][[1]]
## [1] "split london heathrow via warsaw th april firstly split airport one check counter rather online absolutely fine dealt professional friendly manner member split airport staff young lady question professional friendly created great impression company boarding efficiently managed split airport aircraft used  warsaw chopin airport embraer cabincrew took security seriously made sure passengers complied saw cabin crew proactively checking areas seats making sure baggage stored away correctly transfer warsaw airport efficient  staff hand help passengers alas onward  delayed however kept passengers well informed text messages announcements  london immaculate boeing max staff exceptionally professional snack coffee offered shame route map will certainly consider using airline opportunity arise"
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)

# Sprawdzenie
corpus[[1]][[1]]
## [1] "split london heathrow via warsaw th april firstly split airport one check counter rather online absolutely fine dealt professional friendly manner member split airport staff young lady question professional friendly created great impression company boarding efficiently managed split airport aircraft used warsaw chopin airport embraer cabincrew took security seriously made sure passengers complied saw cabin crew proactively checking areas seats making sure baggage stored away correctly transfer warsaw airport efficient staff hand help passengers alas onward delayed however kept passengers well informed text messages announcements london immaculate boeing max staff exceptionally professional snack coffee offered shame route map will certainly consider using airline opportunity arise"
# Decyzja dotyczÄ…ca korpusu ----
# do dalszej analizy uĹĽyj:
#
# - corpus (oryginalny, bez stemmingu)
#

Tokenizacja

# Tokenizacja ----



# Macierz czÄ™stoĹ›ci TDM ----

tdm <- TermDocumentMatrix(corpus)
tdm_m <- as.matrix(tdm)

2. Zliczanie czÄ™stoĹ›ci sĹ‚Ăłw

# 2. Zliczanie czÄ™stoĹ›ci sĹ‚Ăłw ----
# (Word Frequency Count)


# Zlicz same czÄ™stoĹ›ci sĹ‚Ăłw w macierzach
v <- sort(rowSums(tdm_m), decreasing = TRUE)
tdm_df <- data.frame(word = names(v), freq = v)
head(tdm_df, 10)

3. Eksploracyjna analiza danych

# 3. Eksploracyjna analiza danych ----
# (Exploratory Data Analysis, EDA)


# Chmura sĹ‚Ăłw (globalna)
wordcloud(words = tdm_df$word, freq = tdm_df$freq, min.freq = 7, 
          colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2"))

# WyĹ›wietl top 10
print(head(tdm_df, 10))
##              word freq
## service   service   66
## warsaw     warsaw   51
## airline   airline   50
## one           one   49
## seats       seats   44
## even         even   43
## airlines airlines   42
## time         time   42
## staff       staff   36
## airport   airport   35

4. InĹĽynieria cech w modelu Bag of Words:

Reprezentacja sĹ‚Ăłw i dokumentĂłw w przestrzeni wektorowej

# 4. InĹĽynieria cech w modelu Bag of Words: ----
# Reprezentacja sĹ‚Ăłw i dokumentĂłw w przestrzeni wektorowej ----
# (Feature Engineering in vector-space BoW model)

# - podejĹ›cie surowych czÄ™stoĹ›ci sĹ‚Ăłw
# (czÄ™stoĹ›Ä‡ sĹ‚owa = liczba wystÄ…pieĹ„ w dokumencie)
# (Raw Word Counts)

UCZENIE MASZYNOWE NIENADZOROWANE

# UCZENIE MASZYNOWE NIENADZOROWANE ----
# (Unsupervised Machine Learning)

Modelowanie tematĂłw: ukryta alokacja Dirichleta

# Modelowanie tematĂłw: ukryta alokacja Dirichleta (LDA) ----




# Rysuj dziesiÄ™Ä‡ sĹ‚Ăłw 
# o najwiÄ™kszej informatywnoĹ›ci wedĹ‚ug tematu
# dla wyznaczonej liczby tematĂłw 


# DobĂłr liczby tematĂłw
number_of_topics = 2
top_terms_by_topic_LDA(tdm_df$word)

# ZmieĹ„ wyznaczonÄ… liczbÄ™ tematĂłw
number_of_topics = 3
top_terms_by_topic_LDA(tdm_df$word)

# ZmieĹ„ wyznaczonÄ… liczbÄ™ tematĂłw
number_of_topics = 4
top_terms_by_topic_LDA(tdm_df$word)

# ZmieĹ„ wyznaczonÄ… liczbÄ™ tematĂłw
number_of_topics = 6
top_terms_by_topic_LDA(tdm_df$word)
